message:
fix: clippy and fmt fixes across stream/then.rs, e2e_distributed.rs

- Replace loop+match with while-let in stream/then.rs test helper
- Fix clone-on-copy, wildcard-for-single-variant, match-same-arms,
  too-many-lines, and missing-semicollon in e2e_distributed.rs
- Close beads bd-spu1 (not-a-bug) and bd-3pbs (already-fixed)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1ndg","title":"Async I/O Traits Verification Suite (unit tests, adapters)","description":"# Async I/O Traits Verification Suite

## Purpose
Comprehensive verification for async I/O traits (imz, tokio-io equivalent) ensuring correct trait implementations and adapter behavior.

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests
- AsyncRead: poll_read, read_buf
- AsyncWrite: poll_write, poll_flush, poll_shutdown
- AsyncBufRead: poll_fill_buf, consume
- AsyncSeek: poll_complete, start_seek
- Utility: read_to_end, read_to_string, read_exact
- Utility: write_all, copy, duplex

### 2. Adapter Tests
- BufReader: buffered reading
- BufWriter: buffered writing
- BufStream: bidirectional buffering
- Chain: sequential reading
- Take: limited reading
- Split: byte delimiter splitting
- Lines: line iterator
- ReadHalf/WriteHalf: split I/O

### 3. E2E Scenarios
| Scenario | Verifies |
|----------|----------|
| Copy stream | Efficient I/O copy |
| Buffered read chain | Adapter composition |
| Cancel during read_to_end | Partial result safety |
| Duplex echo | Bidirectional I/O |
| Split + join | Reader/writer split |

### 4. Trait Compliance Tests
- Implementors: TcpStream, File, etc.
- Contract verification: flush on drop
- Cancel-safety for all operations

## Logging Requirements
- I/O events logged with buffer sizes
- On failure: dump adapter state
- Byte throughput metrics

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All traits have unit tests
- [ ] All adapters have unit tests
- [ ] E2E scenarios pass
- [ ] Trait compliance verified
- [ ] `cargo test io` passes deterministically

## Verification Commands
```bash
cargo test --package asupersync --lib io
cargo test --test io_e2e
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"FrostyCanyon","created_at":"2026-01-22T19:49:07.382659203Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T04:13:23.906638504Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T04:13:23.906561130Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria met. 835 IO unit tests (AsyncRead, AsyncWrite, AsyncBufRead, adapters: BufReader, BufWriter, Chain, Take, Split), 17 IO E2E tests (read/write, split, cancel, region close, replay determinism), 21 IO cancellation tests (feature-gated). Trait compliance verified across TcpStream, UnixStream, File. All 873 tests pass with 0 failures.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ndg","depends_on_id":"asupersync-imz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:20:45Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1ows","title":"HTTP/3 client: request/response over QUIC","description":"# HTTP/3 Client Implementation

## Overview
HTTP/3 client implementation over QUIC with Cx integration, supporting
request/response patterns with proper stream management.

## Design

### H3Client
```rust
pub struct H3Client {
    conn: QuicConnection,
    h3: h3::client::Connection<QuicConnection, Bytes>,
}

impl H3Client {
    /// Create HTTP/3 client from QUIC connection.
    pub async fn new(cx: &Cx, conn: QuicConnection) -> Outcome<Self, H3Error> {
        cx.checkpoint()?;
        let h3 = h3::client::Connection::new(conn).await?;
        Ok(Self { conn, h3 })
    }
    
    /// Send HTTP request and receive response.
    pub async fn request(
        &mut self,
        cx: &Cx,
        req: Request<()>,
    ) -> Outcome<Response<H3Body>, H3Error> {
        cx.checkpoint()?;
        
        // Send request headers
        let mut stream = self.h3.send_request(req).await?;
        
        // Send body if present
        stream.send_data(/* body */).await?;
        stream.finish().await?;
        
        // Receive response
        let resp = stream.recv_response().await?;
        
        // Create body reader
        let body = H3Body::new(stream);
        
        Ok(resp.map(|_| body))
    }
    
    /// Send request with body.
    pub async fn request_with_body(
        &mut self,
        cx: &Cx,
        req: Request<Bytes>,
    ) -> Outcome<Response<H3Body>, H3Error>;
}
```

### H3Body
```rust
pub struct H3Body {
    stream: h3::client::RequestStream<QuicRecvStream, Bytes>,
    done: bool,
}

impl H3Body {
    /// Read next chunk of response body.
    pub async fn chunk(&mut self, cx: &Cx) -> Outcome<Option<Bytes>, H3Error> {
        cx.checkpoint()?;
        
        if self.done {
            return Ok(None);
        }
        
        match self.stream.recv_data().await? {
            Some(data) => Ok(Some(data)),
            None => {
                self.done = true;
                Ok(None)
            }
        }
    }
    
    /// Read entire body.
    pub async fn collect(mut self, cx: &Cx) -> Outcome<Bytes, H3Error>;
}
```

## Cancellation Semantics
- Request cancellation resets the stream
- Partial responses are discarded
- Connection remains usable for other requests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create H3 client from QUIC connection
- [ ] Send GET requests
- [ ] Send POST requests with body
- [ ] Receive response headers
- [ ] Stream response body
- [ ] Cancellation resets stream
- [ ] Unit tests
- [ ] Integration tests

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- `h3_client::tests::create_client` - Create from QUIC
- `h3_client::tests::get_request` - Simple GET
- `h3_client::tests::post_request` - POST with body
- `h3_client::tests::stream_body` - Stream response body
- `h3_client::tests::collect_body` - Collect full body

### Cancel-Correctness Tests
- `h3_client::cancel::cancel_request` - Cancel mid-request
- `h3_client::cancel::cancel_body_read` - Cancel during body
- `h3_client::cancel::connection_survives` - Other requests work

### Logging Requirements
- TRACE: Frame details
- DEBUG: Request/response flow
- INFO: Request lifecycle
- WARN: Stream errors
- ERROR: Connection errors","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T05:23:10.541874217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T06:49:20.526333339Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T06:49:20.526269841Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ecosystem-parity","http","quic"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ows","depends_on_id":"bd-2tnl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T05:24:30.586067369Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ows","depends_on_id":"bd-2vik","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T05:24:03.917537689Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-1y06","title":"bd-e2e05: e2e::distributed RaptorQ encode/distribute/recover","description":"## E2E Test: Distributed RaptorQ Pipeline

Test the ACTUAL distributed module: encode region state → distribute symbols to replicas → recover from partial symbol sets.

Uses test_phase!, test_section!, assert_with_log!, test_complete! macros.

### Test Phases
1. **Setup**: Create region with tasks, children, budget
2. **Encode**: StateEncoder encodes snapshot into source + repair symbols
3. **Assign**: SymbolAssigner distributes across 3 replicas (Full, Striped, MinimumK)
4. **Distribute**: SymbolDistributor evaluates outcomes (Local, Quorum, All consistency)
5. **Partition**: Remove 1 replica — verify quorum still achievable
6. **Recover**: RecoveryOrchestrator collects symbols → decode snapshot
7. **Verify**: Decoded snapshot matches original
8. **Heal**: Rejoin removed replica → re-sync
9. **Bridge Lifecycle**: RegionBridge Local→Distributed upgrade → snapshot → close

### Detailed Logging
- Phase transitions with timestamps
- Symbol counts per replica
- Quorum status at each check
- Recovery metrics (ESIs collected, duplicates)
- Snapshot field-by-field verification

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Full encode→distribute→recover pipeline
- [ ] Quorum loss + recovery
- [ ] All 3 assignment strategies
- [ ] All 3 consistency levels
- [ ] Bridge upgrade lifecycle
- [ ] test_complete! summary","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T20:18:29.949149853Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T20:18:30.141936505Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critical","distributed","e2e","integration"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1y06","depends_on_id":"bd-13gj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T20:18:30.021640496Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1y06","depends_on_id":"bd-17uj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T20:18:30.059517672Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1y06","depends_on_id":"bd-2lbq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T20:18:29.991430867Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1y06","depends_on_id":"bd-3k9o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T20:18:30.141899025Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1y06","depends_on_id":"bd-fgs0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T20:18:30.102036358Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1yyt","title":"select\! macro: pattern-matching on first completion","description":"# select\! Macro Implementation

## Goal

Implement the select\! macro for pattern-matching on the first future to complete, with ergonomic syntax and proper cancellation handling.

## Semantics

```rust
select\! {
    result = operation_a => {
        println\!('A completed: {:?}', result);
    }
    result = operation_b => {
        println\!('B completed: {:?}', result);
    }
    else => {
        println\!('No operation ready');
    }
}
```

## Key Properties

### 1. Pattern Matching
Unlike race\!, select\! allows arbitrary code in each branch:
- Can destructure result
- Can perform side effects
- Can return different types (if unified)

### 2. Continuation
After one branch completes, the select expression evaluates to that branch's body. Remaining futures are NOT automatically cancelled (unlike race\!).

### 3. Cancellation Control
User controls what happens to non-winning branches:
```rust
select\! {
    a = fut_a => {
        // fut_b still pending, could continue later
        handle_a(a)
    }
    b = fut_b => {
        handle_b(b)
    }
}
// Remaining futures dropped here (Drop, not Cancel)
```

For explicit cancellation:
```rust
select\! {
    cancel_others; // Modifier: cancel losers like race\!
    a = fut_a => handle_a(a),
    b = fut_b => handle_b(b),
}
```

### 4. Else Branch
The `else` branch runs if all futures are pending and would block:
```rust
select\! {
    a = fut_a => handle_a(a),
    b = fut_b => handle_b(b),
    else => {
        // Only runs if both would block
        // Useful for non-blocking check
    }
}
```

### 5. Default Branch
The `default` branch runs if no future is ready (before awaiting):
```rust
select\! {
    a = fut_a => handle_a(a),
    default => {
        // Runs immediately if fut_a not ready
        // Does NOT wait for fut_a
    }
}
```

## Biased Select

```rust
select\! {
    biased; // Always try first branch first
    
    msg = rx.recv() => handle_msg(msg),
    _ = shutdown.recv() => break,
}
```

Without biased, order is randomized to prevent starvation.

## Loop Select Pattern

Common pattern for event loops:
```rust
loop {
    select\! {
        msg = rx.recv() => {
            if process(msg).is_err() {
                break;
            }
        }
        _ = interval.tick() => {
            do_periodic_work();
        }
        _ = shutdown.recv() => {
            break;
        }
    }
}
```

## Implementation

### Macro Expansion
```rust
select\! {
    a = fut_a => expr_a,
    b = fut_b => expr_b,
}
// Expands to:
{
    let mut __fut_a = std::pin::pin\!(fut_a);
    let mut __fut_b = std::pin::pin\!(fut_b);
    
    std::future::poll_fn(|cx| {
        // Poll in random order (or biased order)
        let order = if biased { [0, 1] } else { random_order(2) };
        
        for i in order {
            match i {
                0 => {
                    if let Poll::Ready(a) = __fut_a.as_mut().poll(cx) {
                        return Poll::Ready(expr_a);
                    }
                }
                1 => {
                    if let Poll::Ready(b) = __fut_b.as_mut().poll(cx) {
                        return Poll::Ready(expr_b);
                    }
                }
                _ => unreachable\!(),
            }
        }
        
        // Check for else/default
        Poll::Pending
    }).await
}
```

## Precondition Guards

```rust
select\! {
    a = fut_a, if condition_a => handle_a(a),
    b = fut_b, if condition_b => handle_b(b),
}
```

Only poll branches where guard is true.

## Type Inference

All branches must return the same type (for the overall expression):
```rust
let result: i32 = select\! {
    a = fut_a => a * 2,
    b = fut_b => b + 1,
};
```

## Integration with Asupersync

- Cx available in branch bodies
- Cancellation checkpoints respected
- Obligations from non-taken branches cleaned up on drop

## Testing

- Basic select with 2 branches
- Biased vs random ordering
- Else branch behavior
- Default branch behavior
- Precondition guards
- Loop select pattern
- Type inference
- cancel_others modifier

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] select\! macro with arm syntax
- [ ] Pattern matching in arms
- [ ] biased modifier
- [ ] else branch
- [ ] default branch
- [ ] Precondition guards
- [ ] cancel_others modifier
- [ ] Random ordering (deterministic in lab)
- [ ] Tests for all patterns","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:31:25.654274958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T08:00:10.556705401Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T08:00:10.556474351Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["combinators","core","select"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1yyt","depends_on_id":"bd-14c0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:35:15.614166869Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1yyt","depends_on_id":"bd-1sx3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:31:25.681261661Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1yyt","depends_on_id":"bd-24hd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:35:17.648672746Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1z3g","title":"bd-ut09: signal module comprehensive tests","description":"## Unit Tests for src/signal/ (1496 LOC, 25 existing tests)

Signal module is Phase 0 (not_implemented errors). Tests should verify:

### Test Cases — signal.rs (270 LOC, 3 tests)
- All signal constructors (sigint, sigterm, sighup, sigusr1, sigusr2, sigquit, sigchld, sigwinch) return appropriate SignalError::not_implemented
- SignalError display includes signal name and Phase 0 context
- Signal::recv() on not_implemented signal — verify pending behavior

### Test Cases — graceful.rs (397 LOC, 7 tests)
- GracefulShutdown trigger: first trigger starts shutdown
- GracefulShutdown double-trigger: idempotent
- Shutdown with zero in-flight tasks — immediate completion
- Shutdown with active tasks — waits for drain
- Shutdown timeout: tasks don't finish within deadline

### Test Cases — shutdown.rs (388 LOC, 9 tests)
- Shutdown ordering: registered shutdown hooks execute in order
- Concurrent shutdown trigger from multiple tasks — only one wins
- Shutdown hook panic — other hooks still execute
- Nested shutdown (shutdown during shutdown hook)

### Test Cases — kind.rs (245 LOC, 4 tests)
- SignalKind Display/Debug formatting
- SignalKind from_raw and to_raw round-trip
- Platform-specific signals (SIGWINCH etc.) — conditional compilation

### Logging Requirements
Each test logs: signal kind, operation attempted, result (Ok/Err), phase context.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 15+ new tests across signal submodules
- [ ] Phase 0 error paths fully exercised
- [ ] Graceful shutdown sequencing verified
- [ ] All SignalKind variants covered","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T20:16:07.729495248Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T20:24:18.179077624Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T20:24:18.179013675Z","close_reason":"25 existing tests, signal module is Phase 0 stubs","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["signal","unit-test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1z3g","depends_on_id":"bd-15et","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T20:16:07.751161514Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1z7e","title":"HTTP/2 Security: comprehensive test suite and fuzz tests","description":"# Task: Comprehensive Test Suite for HTTP/2 Security Hardening

## Overview

After fixing the HTTP/2 security vulnerabilities, we need comprehensive testing to:
1. Verify all security fixes work correctly
2. Prevent regression of security issues
3. Fuzz test with malicious inputs
4. Verify compliance with RFC 7540/7541

## Test Categories

### Unit Tests - HPACK Decoding

#### Integer Decoding Tests
- [ ] `test_decode_integer_basic` - Standard integer values (1, 127, 128, 255)
- [ ] `test_decode_integer_max_prefix` - Values at prefix boundary (5-bit, 6-bit, 7-bit)
- [ ] `test_decode_integer_multi_byte` - Multi-byte continuation sequences
- [ ] `test_decode_integer_overflow_shift` - Shift >28 bits rejected with H2Error
- [ ] `test_decode_integer_overflow_addition` - Addition overflow rejected
- [ ] `test_decode_integer_truncated` - Incomplete input rejected (need more data)
- [ ] `test_decode_integer_max_value` - Maximum valid integer accepted
- [ ] `test_decode_integer_leading_zeros` - Redundant continuation bytes handled

#### Integer Encoding Tests
- [ ] `test_encode_integer_basic` - Standard values round-trip correctly
- [ ] `test_encode_integer_prefix_sizes` - All prefix sizes (5, 6, 7, 8 bits)
- [ ] `test_encode_integer_boundary` - Values at 2^N-1 boundaries
- [ ] `test_encode_decode_roundtrip` - Property: encode(decode(x)) == x

#### Huffman Decoding Tests
- [ ] `test_huffman_decode_basic` - Valid encoded strings
- [ ] `test_huffman_decode_invalid_padding` - Wrong padding bits (not all 1s) rejected
- [ ] `test_huffman_decode_truncated_symbol` - Incomplete symbol rejected
- [ ] `test_huffman_decode_eos_symbol` - EOS (0x3fffffff) in data rejected
- [ ] `test_huffman_decode_overlong_padding` - >7 bits padding rejected
- [ ] `test_huffman_empty_string` - Empty input returns empty string
- [ ] `test_huffman_all_symbols` - Full alphabet (0x00-0xFF) coverage
- [ ] `test_huffman_max_symbol_length` - 30-bit symbols decode correctly

#### Huffman Encoding Tests
- [ ] `test_huffman_encode_basic` - Common strings encode correctly
- [ ] `test_huffman_encode_binary` - Binary data with all byte values
- [ ] `test_huffman_encode_decode_roundtrip` - Property: decode(encode(s)) == s
- [ ] `test_huffman_encode_compression_ratio` - Typical text compresses well

#### Static Table Tests
- [ ] `test_static_table_entries` - All 61 entries present and correct
- [ ] `test_static_table_lookup_by_index` - Index lookup returns correct entry
- [ ] `test_static_table_lookup_by_name` - Name lookup returns correct index
- [ ] `test_static_table_pseudoheaders` - :method, :path, :scheme, :status correct
- [ ] `test_static_table_common_headers` - accept-encoding, content-type, etc.

#### Dynamic Table Tests
- [ ] `test_dynamic_table_insert` - Basic insertion (index 62+)
- [ ] `test_dynamic_table_eviction` - FIFO eviction on overflow
- [ ] `test_dynamic_table_size_update` - Size update evicts old entries
- [ ] `test_dynamic_table_max_size_cap` - MAX_ALLOWED_TABLE_SIZE (1MB) enforced
- [ ] `test_dynamic_table_malicious_size` - Giant size updates rejected
- [ ] `test_dynamic_table_sequence_limit` - MAX_SIZE_UPDATES (16) enforced
- [ ] `test_dynamic_table_entry_size` - Entry size = name + value + 32
- [ ] `test_dynamic_table_empty` - Empty table handles lookups gracefully
- [ ] `test_dynamic_table_zero_size` - Zero-size table rejects all inserts

### Unit Tests - Frame Parsing

#### Common Frame Tests
- [ ] `test_frame_header_parse` - 9-byte frame header parsing
- [ ] `test_frame_length_limit` - Frames > 16KB rejected (default MAX_FRAME_SIZE)
- [ ] `test_frame_unknown_type` - Unknown frame types ignored per RFC
- [ ] `test_frame_reserved_flags` - Reserved flags preserved

#### DATA Frame Tests
- [ ] `test_data_basic` - Valid DATA frame
- [ ] `test_data_with_padding` - Padding handled correctly
- [ ] `test_data_padding_exceeds_length` - Padding > payload rejected
- [ ] `test_data_end_stream` - END_STREAM flag terminates stream
- [ ] `test_data_flow_control` - Flow control window checked
- [ ] `test_data_on_idle_stream` - DATA on idle stream rejected

#### HEADERS Frame Tests
- [ ] `test_headers_basic` - Valid HEADERS frame
- [ ] `test_headers_continuation` - Multi-frame header block
- [ ] `test_headers_padding` - Padding handled correctly
- [ ] `test_headers_priority` - Priority data parsed
- [ ] `test_headers_fragment_size_cap` - MAX_HEADER_LIST_SIZE enforced
- [ ] `test_headers_end_headers` - END_HEADERS completes block
- [ ] `test_headers_exclusive_dependency` - Exclusive flag parsed

#### PRIORITY Frame Tests
- [ ] `test_priority_basic` - Valid PRIORITY frame
- [ ] `test_priority_self_dependency` - Self-dependency rejected (PROTOCOL_ERROR)
- [ ] `test_priority_circular` - Circular dependency detection
- [ ] `test_priority_weight_bounds` - Weight 1-256 (0 = 256)
- [ ] `test_priority_stream_zero` - PRIORITY on stream 0 rejected
- [ ] `test_priority_wrong_length` - Length != 5 rejected

#### RST_STREAM Frame Tests
- [ ] `test_rst_stream_basic` - Valid RST_STREAM
- [ ] `test_rst_stream_error_codes` - All error codes parsed correctly
- [ ] `test_rst_stream_stream_zero` - RST_STREAM on stream 0 rejected
- [ ] `test_rst_stream_wrong_length` - Length != 4 rejected
- [ ] `test_rst_stream_idle_stream` - RST_STREAM on idle stream allowed

#### SETTINGS Frame Tests
- [ ] `test_settings_basic` - Valid SETTINGS frame
- [ ] `test_settings_ack` - ACK flag with empty payload
- [ ] `test_settings_ack_nonempty` - ACK with payload rejected
- [ ] `test_settings_stream_zero` - SETTINGS only on stream 0
- [ ] `test_settings_wrong_length` - Length not multiple of 6 rejected
- [ ] `test_settings_header_table_size` - HEADER_TABLE_SIZE limit enforced
- [ ] `test_settings_enable_push` - ENABLE_PUSH 0 or 1 only
- [ ] `test_settings_max_concurrent` - MAX_CONCURRENT_STREAMS enforced
- [ ] `test_settings_initial_window` - INITIAL_WINDOW_SIZE <= 2^31-1
- [ ] `test_settings_max_frame_size` - 16384 <= MAX_FRAME_SIZE <= 16777215
- [ ] `test_settings_max_header_list` - MAX_HEADER_LIST_SIZE advisory
- [ ] `test_settings_unknown_id` - Unknown setting IDs ignored

#### PUSH_PROMISE Frame Tests
- [ ] `test_push_promise_basic` - Valid PUSH_PROMISE
- [ ] `test_push_promise_stream_id_valid` - Promised stream ID even
- [ ] `test_push_promise_reserved_stream` - Stream properly reserved
- [ ] `test_push_promise_max_concurrent` - MAX_CONCURRENT_STREAMS enforced
- [ ] `test_push_promise_cleanup_on_error` - Resources freed on failure
- [ ] `test_push_promise_disabled` - Rejected when ENABLE_PUSH=0
- [ ] `test_push_promise_on_push_stream` - PUSH_PROMISE on pushed stream rejected

#### PING Frame Tests
- [ ] `test_ping_basic` - Valid PING frame
- [ ] `test_ping_ack` - PING ACK echoes opaque data
- [ ] `test_ping_wrong_length` - Length != 8 rejected
- [ ] `test_ping_stream_zero` - PING only on stream 0
- [ ] `test_ping_flood` - Rate limiting (if implemented)

#### GOAWAY Frame Tests
- [ ] `test_goaway_basic` - Valid GOAWAY frame
- [ ] `test_goaway_error_code` - All error codes parsed
- [ ] `test_goaway_debug_data` - Debug data preserved
- [ ] `test_goaway_stream_zero` - GOAWAY only on stream 0
- [ ] `test_goaway_last_stream` - Last-Stream-ID semantics

#### WINDOW_UPDATE Frame Tests
- [ ] `test_window_update_basic` - Valid WINDOW_UPDATE
- [ ] `test_window_update_connection` - Stream 0 updates connection window
- [ ] `test_window_update_stream` - Non-zero stream updates stream window
- [ ] `test_window_update_zero` - Increment 0 rejected (PROTOCOL_ERROR)
- [ ] `test_window_update_overflow` - Window > 2^31-1 causes FLOW_CONTROL_ERROR
- [ ] `test_window_update_wrong_length` - Length != 4 rejected

#### CONTINUATION Frame Tests
- [ ] `test_continuation_valid` - Valid continuation sequence
- [ ] `test_continuation_timeout` - Timeout after CONTINUATION_TIMEOUT_SECS
- [ ] `test_continuation_unexpected` - CONTINUATION without HEADERS rejected
- [ ] `test_continuation_interleaved` - Other frames during continuation rejected
- [ ] `test_continuation_end_headers` - END_HEADERS completes block
- [ ] `test_continuation_stream_mismatch` - Wrong stream ID rejected

### Security Stress Tests

```rust
#[test]
#[ignore]
fn stress_test_hpack_integer_malformed() {
    // 10,000 malformed multi-byte integer sequences
    // Verify no panics, only clean H2Error returns
    // Log: input bytes, error type, decode duration
}

#[test]
#[ignore]
fn stress_test_huffman_random_bytes() {
    // 10,000 random byte sequences (1-1000 bytes)
    // Verify graceful failure or valid decode
    // Log: input length, result type, duration
}

#[test]
#[ignore]
fn stress_test_dynamic_table_churn() {
    // Rapid size updates (0 -> max -> 0 -> max)
    // Interleaved with insertions
    // Verify memory bounded, no leaks
    // Log: table size, entry count, memory usage
}

#[test]
#[ignore]
fn stress_test_continuation_timeout() {
    // Send HEADERS with END_HEADERS=false
    // Never send CONTINUATION
    // Verify connection closed after timeout
    // Log: timeout value, actual wait time
}

#[test]
#[ignore]
fn stress_test_push_promise_flood() {
    // Rapid PUSH_PROMISE frames (1000/sec)
    // Verify MAX_CONCURRENT_STREAMS enforced
    // Verify no resource leaks on rejection
    // Log: accepted count, rejected count, memory
}

#[test]
#[ignore]
fn stress_test_settings_flood() {
    // Rapid SETTINGS frames without ACK
    // Verify pending settings queue bounded
    // Log: pending count, rejection reason
}

#[test]
#[ignore]
fn stress_test_window_update_overflow() {
    // Attempt to overflow flow control windows
    // Verify FLOW_CONTROL_ERROR sent
    // Log: window values, increment attempts
}
```

### Fuzz Test Infrastructure

```rust
// fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_hpack_decode.rs
#![no_main]
use libfuzzer_sys::fuzz_target;
use asupersync::http::h2::hpack::HpackDecoder;

fuzz_target!(|data: &[u8]| {
    let mut decoder = HpackDecoder::new(4096);
    let _ = decoder.decode_header_block(data);
    // No panic = success
});

// fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_frame_parse.rs
fuzz_target!(|data: &[u8]| {
    let _ = Frame::parse(data);
});

// fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_huffman_decode.rs
fuzz_target!(|data: &[u8]| {
    let _ = huffman_decode(data);
});

// fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_settings_frame.rs
fuzz_target!(|data: &[u8]| {
    let _ = SettingsFrame::parse(data);
});
```

### E2E Test Script

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# scripts/test_http2_security_e2e.sh

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
LOG_DIR="$PROJECT_ROOT/test_logs/http2_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)"
mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"

echo "=== HTTP/2 Security E2E Test Suite ==="
echo "Log directory: $LOG_DIR"
echo "Start time: $(date -Iseconds)"

# Run HPACK unit tests
echo ""
echo "[1/7] Running HPACK unit tests..."
RUST_LOG=debug cargo test --lib h2::hpack -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/hpack_tests.log"
HPACK_EXIT=${PIPESTATUS[0]}

# Run frame parsing tests
echo ""
echo "[2/7] Running frame parsing tests..."
RUST_LOG=debug cargo test --lib h2::frame -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/frame_tests.log"
FRAME_EXIT=${PIPESTATUS[0]}

# Run connection state machine tests
echo ""
echo "[3/7] Running connection tests..."
RUST_LOG=debug cargo test --lib h2::connection -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/connection_tests.log"
CONNECTION_EXIT=${PIPESTATUS[0]}

# Run security stress tests
echo ""
echo "[4/7] Running security stress tests (timeout: 300s)..."
timeout 300s cargo test --lib --release h2_security_stress -- --ignored --nocapture 2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/stress_tests.log"
STRESS_EXIT=${PIPESTATUS[0]}

# Run fuzz tests (limited iterations for CI)
echo ""
echo "[5/7] Running fuzz tests (60s each target)..."
if command -v cargo-fuzz &>/dev/null; then
    for target in fuzz_hpack_decode fuzz_frame_parse fuzz_huffman_decode fuzz_settings_frame; do
        echo "Fuzzing $target..."
        timeout 60s cargo +nightly fuzz run "$target" -- -max_total_time=60 2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/fuzz_${target}.log" || true
    done
    FUZZ_EXIT=0
else
    echo "cargo-fuzz not available, skipping fuzz tests"
    FUZZ_EXIT=0
fi

# Check for memory leaks with valgrind
echo ""
echo "[6/7] Checking for memory leaks..."
if command -v valgrind &>/dev/null; then
    cargo test --lib h2::hpack::tests --release --no-run 2>/dev/null
    TEST_BIN=$(find target/release/deps -name "asupersync-*" -type f -executable | head -1)
    if [ -n "$TEST_BIN" ]; then
        valgrind --leak-check=full --error-exitcode=1 "$TEST_BIN" --test hpack 2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/valgrind.log" || true
    fi
else
    echo "Valgrind not available, skipping"
fi

# Generate summary
echo ""
echo "[7/7] Generating summary..."
cat > "$LOG_DIR/summary.md" << EOF
# HTTP/2 Security Test Report

## Date: $(date -Iseconds)

## Test Results

| Suite | Status |
|-------|--------|
| HPACK | $([ $HPACK_EXIT -eq 0 ] && echo "✅ PASS" || echo "❌ FAIL") |
| Frame Parsing | $([ $FRAME_EXIT -eq 0 ] && echo "✅ PASS" || echo "❌ FAIL") |
| Connection | $([ $CONNECTION_EXIT -eq 0 ] && echo "✅ PASS" || echo "❌ FAIL") |
| Stress Tests | $([ $STRESS_EXIT -eq 0 ] && echo "✅ PASS" || echo "❌ FAIL") |
| Fuzz Tests | $([ $FUZZ_EXIT -eq 0 ] && echo "✅ PASS" || echo "⚠️ SKIPPED") |

## Failures

$(grep -hE "(FAILED|panicked|ERROR)" "$LOG_DIR"/*.log 2>/dev/null || echo "None")

## Coverage

$(grep -hE "^test result:" "$LOG_DIR"/*.log 2>/dev/null || echo "N/A")
EOF

cat "$LOG_DIR/summary.md"

echo ""
echo "End time: $(date -Iseconds)"
echo "Logs saved to: $LOG_DIR"
echo "=== Test Complete ==="

# Exit with failure if critical tests failed
[ $HPACK_EXIT -eq 0 ] && [ $FRAME_EXIT -eq 0 ] && [ $CONNECTION_EXIT -eq 0 ] && [ $STRESS_EXIT -eq 0 ]
```

## RFC Compliance Verification

- [ ] Integer encoding per RFC 7541 Section 5.1
- [ ] Huffman coding per RFC 7541 Appendix B
- [ ] Static table per RFC 7541 Appendix A
- [ ] Dynamic table per RFC 7541 Section 2.3
- [ ] Frame format per RFC 7540 Section 4
- [ ] PUSH_PROMISE per RFC 7540 Section 6.6
- [ ] CONTINUATION per RFC 7540 Section 6.10
- [ ] SETTINGS per RFC 7540 Section 6.5
- [ ] Flow control per RFC 7540 Section 5.2

## Logging Requirements

```rust
tracing::debug!(
    test = "hpack_decode",
    input_len = data.len(),
    table_size = decoder.table_size(),
    "Decoding header block"
);

tracing::info!(
    test = "frame_parse",
    frame_type = ?frame.frame_type(),
    stream_id = frame.stream_id(),
    flags = frame.flags(),
    length = frame.length(),
    "Parsed frame"
);

tracing::warn!(
    test = "security_reject",
    reason = "integer overflow",
    shift = shift_amount,
    value = current_value,
    "Rejecting malformed input"
);
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 80+ unit tests implemented and passing
- [ ] All frame types covered with positive and negative tests
- [ ] Stress tests run 5+ minutes without failure
- [ ] Fuzz tests run 1 hour without crashes
- [ ] No memory leaks under attack scenarios
- [ ] RFC compliance verified and documented
- [ ] E2E script runs in CI with proper exit codes

 {"id":"bd-hszn","title":"gRPC Framework Verification Suite (unit tests, E2E, streaming)","description":"# gRPC Framework Verification Suite

## Purpose
Comprehensive verification for the gRPC framework (3or, tonic equivalent) ensuring protocol compliance, streaming correctness, and cancel-safety.

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests
- Service definitions: codegen output
- Request/Response types: encoding, decoding
- Metadata: headers, trailers
- Status codes: mapping, propagation
- Interceptors: client and server

### 2. Streaming Pattern Tests
- Unary: single request, single response
- Server streaming: single request, stream response
- Client streaming: stream request, single response
- Bidirectional: stream request, stream response

### 3. E2E Scenarios
| Scenario | Verifies |
|----------|----------|
| Unary RPC | Basic request/response |
| Server streaming | Large result set |
| Client streaming | Upload pattern |
| Bidirectional chat | Real-time communication |
| Deadline propagation | Timeout inheritance |
| Cancel mid-stream | Clean termination |
| Metadata round-trip | Header/trailer handling |
| Reflection service | Service discovery |

### 4. Protocol Compliance Tests
- gRPC over HTTP/2
- Message framing
- Compression: gzip, deflate, none
- Content-Type validation
- Status code mapping

### 5. Load Tests
- Concurrent RPCs
- Long-running streams
- Connection multiplexing
- Backpressure handling

### 6. Security Tests
- TLS integration
- mTLS client auth
- Token validation

## Logging Requirements
- RPC events logged with method names
- On failure: dump message state, stream state
- Timing: request/response latency per RPC

 {"id":"bd-ifn2","title":"HTTP/3 E2E test suite: QUIC transport and HTTP/3 protocol tests","description":"# HTTP/3 E2E Test Suite

## Overview
Comprehensive end-to-end test suite for HTTP/3 implementation covering
QUIC transport, HTTP/3 protocol, and cancel-correctness.

## Test Directory Structure
```
tests/e2e/h3/
├── mod.rs                       # Test module root
├── common/
│   ├── mod.rs                   # Shared utilities
│   ├── certs.rs                 # Test certificates
│   └── fixtures.rs              # Test data
├── quic/
│   ├── endpoint.rs              # Endpoint tests
│   ├── connection.rs            # Connection tests
│   ├── streams.rs               # Stream tests
│   └── cancel.rs                # Cancellation tests
├── h3/
│   ├── client.rs                # Client tests
│   ├── server.rs                # Server tests
│   ├── headers.rs               # Header handling
│   ├── body.rs                  # Body streaming
│   └── cancel.rs                # Cancellation tests
├── interop/
│   ├── curl.rs                  # Interop with curl
│   ├── browser.rs               # Browser compatibility
│   └── quic_go.rs               # Go QUIC server
└── lab/
    ├── deterministic.rs         # Lab runtime tests
    └── loss_simulation.rs       # Packet loss tests
```

## Test Categories

### 1. QUIC Transport Tests
- Endpoint creation and binding
- Connection establishment
- 0-RTT resumption
- Stream creation and data transfer
- Connection migration (optional)

### 2. HTTP/3 Protocol Tests
- Request/response round-trip
- Multiple concurrent requests
- Header compression (QPACK)
- Body streaming
- Trailers

### 3. Cancel-Correctness Tests
- Cancel during QUIC handshake
- Cancel during request
- Cancel during response body
- Server shutdown with active requests
- Connection close with pending streams

### 4. Interoperability Tests
- curl --http3 compatibility
- Browser compatibility (Chrome, Firefox)
- Go quic-go server

### 5. Lab Runtime Tests
- Deterministic packet ordering
- Simulated packet loss
- Simulated latency

## Logging Requirements
- TRACE: Packet-level details
- DEBUG: Stream/request flow
- INFO: Test progress
- WARN: Protocol anomalies
- ERROR: Test failures

## Test Scripts

### Run All HTTP/3 Tests
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
export RUST_LOG="asupersync=debug,test=info"

echo "=== HTTP/3 E2E Tests ==="
cargo test -p asupersync --test e2e_h3 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture 2>&1 | tee h3_tests.log
```

### Run Interop Tests
```bash
# Requires curl with HTTP/3 support
cargo test -p asupersync --test e2e_h3 interop:: -- --nocapture
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All QUIC transport tests pass
- [ ] All HTTP/3 protocol tests pass
- [ ] Cancel-correctness verified
- [ ] Interop tests with curl/browsers
- [ ] Lab runtime tests deterministic
- [ ] Structured logging
- [ ] CI integration","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T05:23:41.851091448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T06:50:08.920501867Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T06:50:08.920421828Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","ecosystem-parity","http","quic"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ifn2","depends_on_id":"bd-1ows","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T05:24:39.196228537Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ifn2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vik","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T05:24:12.068202726Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ifn2","depends_on_id":"bd-yeg2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T05:24:43.251126505Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ijg1","title":"RwLock: document writer-preference starvation characteristics","description":"# Documentation: RwLock Writer-Preference Starvation Characteristics

## Location
`src/sync/rwlock.rs:214-217, 256, 270-276`

## Issue Description

The RwLock implementation is writer-preferring (when writers are waiting, new readers 
are blocked). This is a valid design choice, but the starvation characteristics are 
not documented.

## Current Behavior

```rust
// Line 214: New readers blocked when writers waiting
if !state.writer_active && state.writer_waiters == 0 {
    // Only allow read if no writers waiting
}
```

When a writer is waiting:
1. New read requests are blocked
2. Existing readers continue to hold their locks
3. Writer waits for all readers to release
4. Under continuous read pressure, writer can wait indefinitely

## Starvation Scenario

```
Time    Readers     Writer
────    ───────     ──────
0       R1, R2      -
1       R1, R2      W1 waiting (blocked by R1, R2)
2       R1          W1 waiting (R2 released, R3 blocked)
3       R3 arrives, blocked
4       R1 releases, W1 acquires
5       W1 releases, R3 acquires
```

This is correct behavior, but readers can starve if writes are frequent.

## Documentation Needed

1. **Module-level docs**: Explain writer-preference policy
2. **RwLock struct docs**: Document fairness characteristics
3. **Examples**: Show typical usage patterns
4. **Performance notes**: When to prefer Mutex vs RwLock

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Module-level documentation updated
- [ ] RwLock struct docs include fairness section
- [ ] Example showing when RwLock is appropriate
- [ ] Starvation characteristics clearly explained","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T20:53:27.910154569Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T07:43:10.151977895Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T07:43:10.151835681Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","runtime","sync"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ijg1","depends_on_id":"bd-10qj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:00:50.375588009Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ijg1","depends_on_id":"bd-3ses","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:00:48.282027887Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ijg1","depends_on_id":"bd-qhai","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T20:53:27.919790063Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
